title: rudbeckia subtomentosa f. craigii
intro: Rudbeckia subtomentosa f. craigii, also known as Prairie coneflower, is a flowering plant species native to the prairies of the Midwestern United States. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, the plant contains bioactive compounds, including sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic acids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Prairie coneflower have been used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, wounds, and respiratory infections, making it a valuable herbal remedy for maintaining overall health and well-being. Prairie coneflower can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, allowing for versatile and effective herbal preparations.
